unresolved.title: The new studio from two former King execs, Queen Digital Entertainment, has closed
unresolved.summary: Update: Knutsson has now confirmed the closure to business publication Breakit. His statement, translated from Swedish, reads: "We are currently planning to close the business. We do not see the economics of scaling mobile gaming in today's market..."

unresolved.title: New game digest: Guild Wars, Zynga’s board game adventure, Scopely’s next game, UFC, The Traitors, more
unresolved.summary: There's also Netflix's new horror game, a Guild Wars spin-off and a couple of interesting indie games in this week's round-up.

unresolved.title: On the podcast: Google Play store fees, Kingshot’s World Cup ads, AppsFlyer’s gets $1bn, Kabam layoffs, ex-King execs kill Queen, more
unresolved.summary: This week’s inside view of the mobile game business.

unresolved.title: Google Play opens up billing options and lowers store fees from June 30
unresolved.summary: US, EU and UK developers get more payment flexibility and lower store fees on the Play Store from June 30, with other territories to follow.

unresolved.title: Data digest: World Cup numbers, DTC hits $17bn, UEFN payouts, May’s top game ads, more
unresolved.summary: There's also milestones for Rush Royale, Dessert DIY, a report on app store security and a new studio fund from Nexon in this week's round-up.

unresolved.title: Kingshot maker Century hires a flurry of footballers for World Cup ad spots
unresolved.summary: Harry Maguire, Michael Owen, John Terry, transfer influencer Fabrizio Romano and Brazilian stars Gabriel Jesus, Jorginho, Marquinhos and Thiago Silva are promoting Century's hit 4X strategy game.

unresolved.title: Honkai: Nexus Anima is Hoyoverse’s take on the Pokémon formula
unresolved.summary: The creature-collecting adventure has a beta test coming on July 9 across iOS, Android and PC.

unresolved.title: Google, Unity, Meta and Moloco partner to invest $1bn in AppsFlyer
unresolved.summary: The ad measurement firm says it will remain “neutral” and “independent” as part of the deal. Axios reported the $1bn figure, which values AppsFlyer at around $2.7bn.

unresolved.title: Prime Minister simulator No. 10: Full Confidence spikes as Keir Starmer quits
unresolved.summary: Indie developer Ben Brewis says his political satire has “blown up in the US and UK charts” amid the unfolding turmoil on Downing Street.
